for who is he who has had boldness to approach to me?” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“You shall be my people,
and I will be your God.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Behold, Yahweh’s storm, his wrath, has gone out,
a sweeping storm:
it will burst on the head of the wicked.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
The fierce anger of Yahweh will not return until he has accomplished,
and until he has performed the intentions of his heart.
In the latter days you will understand it.”
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

explainer.summary: God will make a new covenant with Israel: not written on stone tablets but on their hearts. 'I will be their God, and they will be my people.' Rachel weeps for her children, but there is hope for the future.
themes: The new covenant on the heart
explainer.contextInBook: The new covenant prophecy (vv.31-34) is quoted at length in Hebrews 8 and is the theological foundation of the Lord's Supper.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A new binding agreement between God and His people
wordStudies.meaning: A new binding agreement between God and His people. Unlike the Sinai covenant written on stone, this one is written on the heart.
wordStudies.significance: The new covenant is Jeremiah's most important theological contribution: internalized law, universal knowledge of God, and complete forgiveness.

wordStudies.translatedAs: Love that extends beyond time -- eternal, unfailin
wordStudies.meaning: Love that extends beyond time -- eternal, unfailing, without expiration.
wordStudies.significance: God's love for Israel is not temporary or conditional but everlasting -- it survives exile, destruction, and every form of unfaithfulness.

verses.text: “At that time,” says Yahweh, “I will be the God of all the families of Israel, and they will be my people.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh says, “The people who survive the sword found favor in the wilderness; even Israel, when I went to cause him to rest.”
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text:
Yahweh appeared of old to me, saying,
“Yes, I have loved you with an everlasting love.
Therefore I have drawn you with loving kindness.
verses.explainer: 'I have loved you with an everlasting love; I have drawn you with unfailing kindness.' God's love is not earned but eternal and actively pursuing.

verses.text:
I will build you again,
and you will be built, O virgin of Israel.
You will again be adorned with your tambourines,
and will go out in the dances of those who make merry.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Again you will plant vineyards on the mountains of Samaria.
The planters will plant,
and will enjoy its fruit.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
For there will be a day that the watchmen on the hills of Ephraim cry,
‘Arise, and let us go up to Zion to Yahweh our God.’”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
For Yahweh says,
“Sing with gladness for Jacob,
and shout for the chief of the nations.
Publish, praise, and say,
‘Yahweh, save your people,
the remnant of Israel!’
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text:
Behold, I will bring them from the north country,
and gather them from the uttermost parts of the earth,
along with the blind and the lame,
the woman with child and her who travails with child together.
They will return as a great company.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
They will come with weeping.
I will lead them with petitions.
I will cause them to walk by rivers of waters,
in a straight way in which they won’t stumble;
for I am a father to Israel.
Ephraim is my firstborn.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“Hear Yahweh’s word, you nations,
and declare it in the distant islands. Say,
‘He who scattered Israel will gather him,
and keep him, as a shepherd does his flock.’
verses.explainer: The 'shepherds' are Judah's leaders -- kings, priests, and prophets who were supposed to guide the people but instead led them astray.

verses.text:
For Yahweh has ransomed Jacob,
and redeemed him from the hand of him who was stronger than he.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
They will come and sing in the height of Zion,
and will flow to the goodness of Yahweh,
to the grain, to the new wine, to the oil,
and to the young of the flock and of the herd.
Their soul will be as a watered garden.
They will not sorrow any more at all.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.
The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
I will satiate the soul of the priests with fatness,
and my people will be satisfied with my goodness,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Yahweh says:
“A voice is heard in Ramah,
lamentation and bitter weeping,
Rachel weeping for her children.
She refuses to be comforted for her children,
because they are no more.”
verses.explainer: 'Rachel weeping for her children and refusing to be comforted, because they are no more.' Matthew applies this to Herod's slaughter of infants in Bethlehem.

verses.text:
Yahweh says:
“Refrain your voice from weeping,
and your eyes from tears;
for your work will be rewarded,” says Yahweh.
“They will come again from the land of the enemy.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text:
There is hope for your latter end,” says Yahweh.
“Your children will come again to their own territory.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“I have surely heard Ephraim grieving thus,
‘You have chastised me,
and I was chastised, as an untrained calf.
Turn me, and I will be turned;
for you are Yahweh my God.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Surely after that I was turned.
I repented.
After that I was instructed.
I struck my thigh.
I was ashamed, yes, even confounded,
because I bore the reproach of my youth.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Is Ephraim my dear son?
Is he a darling child?
For as often as I speak against him,
I still earnestly remember him.
therefore my heart yearns for him.
I will surely have mercy on him,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

verses.text:
“Set up road signs.
Make guideposts.
Set your heart toward the highway,
even the way by which you went.
Turn again, virgin of Israel.
Turn again to these your cities.
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

verses.text:
How long will you go here and there,
you backsliding daughter?
For Yahweh has created a new thing in the earth:
a woman will encompass a man.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: “Yet again they will use this speech in the land of Judah and in its cities, when I reverse their captivity: ‘Yahweh bless you, habitation of righteousness, mountain of holiness.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Judah and all its cities will dwell therein together, the farmers, and those who go about with flocks.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: For I have satiated the weary soul, and I have replenished every sorrowful soul.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: On this I awakened, and saw; and my sleep was sweet to me.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Behold, the days come,” says Yahweh, “that I will sow the house of Israel and the house of Judah with the seed of man and with the seed of animal.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: It will happen that, like as I have watched over them to pluck up and to break down and to overthrow and to destroy and to afflict, so will I watch over them to build and to plant,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“In those days they will say no more,
“‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes,
and the children’s teeth are set on edge.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: But everyone will die for his own iniquity. Every man who eats the sour grapes, his teeth will be set on edge.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“Behold, the days come,” says Yahweh,
“that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel,
and with the house of Judah:
verses.explainer: 'The days are coming,' declares the Lord, 'when I will make a new covenant with the people of Israel.' The most important prophetic promise in Jeremiah -- a new kind of relationship with God.

crossReferences.connection: The book of Hebrews quotes this passage at length as the prophecy fulfilled by Jesus's death and resurrection.
Jesus said: 'This cup is the new covenant in my blood' -- directly claiming to inaugurate Jeremiah's new covenant.

thenVsNow.then: The new covenant is the theological bridge between Old and New Testaments -- what Jeremiah prophesied, Jesus enacted.
thenVsNow.now: The new covenant is the theological bridge between Old and New Testaments -- what Jeremiah prophesied, Jesus enacted.

verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text:
“But this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days,”
says Yahweh:
I will put my law in their inward parts,
and I will write it in their heart.
I will be their God,
and they shall be my people.
verses.explainer: 'I will put my law in their minds and write it on their hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.' The new covenant internalizes what the old covenant externalized.

crossReferences.connection: Paul says Christians are 'a letter from Christ, written not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.'
Ezekiel's parallel promise: 'I will give you a new heart and put a new spirit in you.'

verses.explainer: 'They will all know me, from the least of them to the greatest. For I will forgive their wickedness and will remember their sins no more.' Complete knowledge and complete forgiveness.

verses.text:
Yahweh, who gives the sun for a light by day,
and the ordinances of the moon and of the stars for a light by night,
who stirs up the sea, so that its waves roar;
Yahweh of Armies is his name, says:
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“If these ordinances depart from before me,” says Yahweh,
“then the offspring of Israel also will cease from being a nation before me forever.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
Yahweh says: “If heaven above can be measured,
and the foundations of the earth searched out beneath,
then I will also cast off all the offspring of Israel for all that they have done,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: “Behold, the days come,” says Yahweh, “that the city will be built to Yahweh from the tower of Hananel to the gate of the corner.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: The measuring line will go out further straight onward to the hill Gareb, and will turn toward Goah.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: The whole valley of the dead bodies and of the ashes, and all the fields to the brook Kidron, to the corner of the horse gate toward the east, will be holy to Yahweh. It will not be plucked up or thrown down any more forever.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

explainer.summary: While Jerusalem is under siege, God tells Jeremiah to buy a field in Anathoth as a prophetic sign that 'houses, fields, and vineyards will again be bought in this land.' An act of hope during destruction.
explainer.contextInBook: Buying real estate during a siege was an audacious act of faith in God's promise of restoration.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: The word that came to Jeremiah from Yahweh in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah, which was the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Now at that time the king of Babylon’s army was besieging Jerusalem. Jeremiah the prophet was shut up in the court of the guard, which was in the king of Judah’s house.
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: For Zedekiah king of Judah had shut him up, saying, “Why do you prophesy, and say, ‘Yahweh says, “Behold, I will give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he will take it;
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: and Zedekiah king of Judah won’t escape out of the hand of the Chaldeans, but will surely be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon, and will speak with him mouth to mouth, and his eyes will see his eyes;
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: and he will bring Zedekiah to Babylon, and he will be there until I visit him,” says Yahweh: “though you fight with the Chaldeans, you will not prosper?”’”
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: Jeremiah said, “Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: ‘Behold, Hanamel the son of Shallum your uncle will come to you, saying, “Buy my field that is in Anathoth; for the right of redemption is yours to buy it.”’”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“So Hanamel my uncle’s son came to me in the court of the guard according to Yahweh’s word, and said to me, ‘Please buy my field that is in Anathoth, which is in the land of Benjamin; for the right of inheritance is yours, and the redemption is yours. Buy it for yourself.’
“Then I knew that this was Yahweh’s word.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: I bought the field that was in Anathoth of Hanamel my uncle’s son, and weighed him the money, even seventeen shekels of silver.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: I signed the deed, sealed it, called witnesses, and weighed the money in the balances to him.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: So I took the deed of the purchase, both that which was sealed, containing the terms and conditions, and that which was open;
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: and I delivered the deed of the purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah, the son of Mahseiah, in the presence of Hanamel my uncle’s son, and in the presence of the witnesses who subscribed the deed of the purchase, before all the Jews who sat in the court of the guard.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “I commanded Baruch before them, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says: ‘Take these deeds, this deed of the purchase which is sealed, and this deed which is open, and put them in an earthen vessel, that they may last many days.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: For Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel says: ‘Houses and fields and vineyards will yet again be bought in this land.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Now after I had delivered the deed of the purchase to Baruch the son of Neriah, I prayed to Yahweh, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Ah Lord Yahweh! Behold, you have made the heavens and the earth by your great power and by your outstretched arm. There is nothing too hard for you,
verses.explainer: 'Ah, Sovereign Lord, you have made the heavens and the earth by your great power. Nothing is too hard for you.' Jeremiah's prayer of faith while buying a field during a siege.

verses.text: who show loving kindness to thousands, and repay the iniquity of the fathers into the bosom of their children after them; the great, the mighty God, Yahweh of Armies is his name;
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: great in counsel, and mighty in work; whose eyes are open to all the ways of the sons of men, to give everyone according to his ways, and according to the fruit of his doings;
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: who performed signs and wonders in the land of Egypt, even to this day, both in Israel and among other men; and made yourself a name, as it is today;
verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text: and brought your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs, with wonders, with a strong hand, with an outstretched arm, and with great terror;
verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text: and gave them this land, which you swore to their fathers to give them, a land flowing with milk and honey.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: They came in and possessed it, but they didn’t obey your voice and didn’t walk in your law. They have done nothing of all that you commanded them to do. Therefore you have caused all this evil to come upon them.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Behold, siege ramps have come to the city to take it. The city is given into the hand of the Chaldeans who fight against it, because of the sword, of the famine, and of the pestilence. What you have spoken has happened. Behold, you see it.
verses.explainer: Sword, famine, and plague are Jeremiah's recurring trio of judgment -- the standard consequences of prolonged siege warfare.

verses.text: You have said to me, Lord Yahweh, ‘Buy the field for money, and call witnesses;’ whereas the city is given into the hand of the Chaldeans.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Then Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Behold, I am Yahweh, the God of all flesh. Is there anything too hard for me?
verses.explainer: 'I am the Lord, the God of all mankind. Is anything too hard for me?' God's response to Jeremiah's prayer -- rhetorical question with an obvious answer.

verses.text: Therefore Yahweh says: Behold, I will give this city into the hand of the Chaldeans, and into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and he will take it.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: The Chaldeans, who fight against this city, will come and set this city on fire, and burn it with the houses on whose roofs they have offered incense to Baal, and poured out drink offerings to other gods, to provoke me to anger.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “For the children of Israel and the children of Judah have done only that which was evil in my sight from their youth; for the children of Israel have only provoked me to anger with the work of their hands, says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: For this city has been to me a provocation of my anger and of my wrath from the day that they built it even to this day, so that I should remove it from before my face,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: because of all the evil of the children of Israel and of the children of Judah, which they have done to provoke me to anger: they, their kings, their princes, their priests, their prophets, the men of Judah, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: They have turned their backs to me, and not their faces. Although I taught them, rising up early and teaching them, yet they have not listened to receive instruction.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: But they set their abominations in the house which is called by my name, to defile it.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: They built the high places of Baal, which are in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to cause their sons and their daughters to pass through fire to Molech; which I didn’t command them. It didn’t even come into my mind, that they should do this abomination, to cause Judah to sin.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Now therefore Yahweh, the God of Israel, says concerning this city, about which you say, “It is given into the hand of the king of Babylon by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence:”
verses.explainer: Sword, famine, and plague are Jeremiah's recurring trio of judgment -- the standard consequences of prolonged siege warfare.

verses.text: “Behold, I will gather them out of all the countries where I have driven them in my anger, and in my wrath, and in great indignation; and I will bring them again to this place. I will cause them to dwell safely.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Then they will be my people, and I will be their God.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: I will give them one heart and one way, that they may fear me forever, for their good, and the good of their children after them.
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

verses.text: I will make an everlasting covenant with them, that I will not turn away from following them, to do them good. I will put my fear in their hearts, that they may not depart from me.
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

verses.text: Yes, I will rejoice over them to do them good, and I will plant them in this land assuredly with my whole heart and with my whole soul.”
verses.explainer: The heart in Jeremiah is the center of human decision-making and loyalty -- the very place where the new covenant will be written.

verses.text: For Yahweh says: “Just as I have brought all this great evil on this people, so will I bring on them all the good that I have promised them.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: Fields will be bought in this land, about which you say, ‘It is desolate, without man or animal. It is given into the hand of the Chaldeans.’
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Men will buy fields for money, sign the deeds, seal them, and call witnesses, in the land of Benjamin, and in the places around Jerusalem, in the cities of Judah, in the cities of the hill country, in the cities of the lowland, and in the cities of the South; for I will cause their captivity to be reversed,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

explainer.summary: God promises to restore Jerusalem and Judah: 'Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things.' A righteous Branch will spring from David's line.
themes: Call to Me and I will answer
explainer.contextInBook: The Book of Consolation concludes with promises that parallel and reinforce chapters 30-31.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: Moreover Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah the second time, while he was still locked up in the court of the guard, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Yahweh who does it, Yahweh who forms it to establish it—Yahweh is his name, says:
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: ‘Call to me, and I will answer you, and will show you great and difficult things, which you don’t know.’
verses.explainer: 'Call to me and I will answer you and tell you great and unsearchable things you do not know.' An open invitation to prayer with the promise of revelation.

crossReferences.connection: Jesus said: 'Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.'

verses.text: For Yahweh, the God of Israel, says concerning the houses of this city and concerning the houses of the kings of Judah, which are broken down to make a defense against the mounds and against the sword;
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: while men come to fight with the Chaldeans, and to fill them with the dead bodies of men, whom I have killed in my anger and in my wrath, and for all whose wickedness I have hidden my face from this city:
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: ‘Behold, I will bring it health and cure, and I will cure them; and I will reveal to them abundance of peace and truth.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: I will cause the captivity of Judah and the captivity of Israel to return, and will build them, as at the first.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: I will cleanse them from all their iniquity by which they have sinned against me. I will pardon all their iniquities by which they have sinned against me and by which they have transgressed against me.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: This city will be to me for a name of joy, for praise, and for glory, before all the nations of the earth, which will hear all the good that I do to them, and will fear and tremble for all the good and for all the peace that I provide to it.’”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh says: “Yet again there will be heard in this place, about which you say, ‘It is waste, without man and without animal, even in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem, that are desolate, without man and without inhabitant and without animal,’
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: the voice of joy and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride, the voice of those who say, ‘Give thanks to Yahweh of Armies, for Yahweh is good, for his loving kindness endures forever;’ who bring thanksgiving into Yahweh’s house. For I will cause the captivity of the land to be reversed as at the first,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh of Armies says: “Yet again there will be in this place, which is waste, without man and without animal, and in all its cities, a habitation of shepherds causing their flocks to lie down.
verses.explainer: The 'shepherds' are Judah's leaders -- kings, priests, and prophets who were supposed to guide the people but instead led them astray.

verses.text: In the cities of the hill country, in the cities of the lowland, in the cities of the South, in the land of Benjamin, in the places around Jerusalem, and in the cities of Judah, the flocks will again pass under the hands of him who counts them,” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Behold, the days come,” says Yahweh, “that I will perform that good word which I have spoken concerning the house of Israel and concerning the house of Judah.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
“In those days and at that time,
I will cause a Branch of righteousness to grow up to David.
He will execute justice and righteousness in the land.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text:
In those days Judah will be saved,
and Jerusalem will dwell safely.
This is the name by which she will be called:
Yahweh our righteousness.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: For Yahweh says: “David will never lack a man to sit on the throne of the house of Israel.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: The priests the Levites won’t lack a man before me to offer burnt offerings, to burn meal offerings, and to do sacrifice continually.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Yahweh says: ‘If you can break my covenant of the day and my covenant of the night, so that there will not be day and night in their time;
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: then may my covenant also be broken with David my servant, that he won’t have a son to reign on his throne; and with the Levites the priests, my ministers.
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: As the army of the sky can’t be counted, and the sand of the sea can’t be measured; so I will multiply the offspring of David my servant and the Levites who minister to me.’”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: “Don’t consider what this people has spoken, saying, ‘Has Yahweh cast off the two families which he chose?’ Thus they despise my people, that they should be no more a nation before them.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

verses.text: Yahweh says: “If my covenant of day and night fails, if I have not appointed the ordinances of heaven and earth;
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: then I will also cast away the offspring of Jacob, and of David my servant, so that I will not take of his offspring to be rulers over the offspring of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob: for I will cause their captivity to be reversed and will have mercy on them.”
verses.explainer: The Book of Consolation: in the midst of destruction, God promises restoration, a new covenant, and a future beyond exile.

explainer.summary: Zedekiah briefly freed Hebrew slaves during the siege (as the law required) but then re-enslaved them. God pronounces judgment for this covenant violation.
explainer.contextInBook: The re-enslavement of freed slaves exemplifies Judah's pattern of making and breaking promises to God.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, with all his army, all the kingdoms of the earth that were under his dominion, and all the peoples, were fighting against Jerusalem and against all its cities, saying:
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘Go, and speak to Zedekiah king of Judah, and tell him, Yahweh says, “Behold, I will give this city into the hand of the king of Babylon and he will burn it with fire.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: You won’t escape out of his hand, but will surely be taken and delivered into his hand. Your eyes will see the eyes of the king of Babylon, and he will speak with you mouth to mouth. You will go to Babylon.”’
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: “Yet hear Yahweh’s word, O Zedekiah king of Judah: Yahweh says concerning you, ‘You won’t die by the sword.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: You will die in peace; and with the burnings of your fathers, the former kings who were before you, so they will make a burning for you. They will lament you, saying, “Ah Lord!” for I have spoken the word,’ says Yahweh.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah in Jerusalem,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: when the king of Babylon’s army was fighting against Jerusalem, and against all the cities of Judah that were left, against Lachish and against Azekah; for these alone remained of the cities of Judah as fortified cities.
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: The word came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, after king Zedekiah had made a covenant with all the people who were at Jerusalem, to proclaim liberty to them;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: that every man should let his male servant, and every man his female servant, who is a Hebrew or a Hebrewess, go free; that no one should make bondservants of them, of a Jew his brother.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: All the princes and all the people obeyed who had entered into the covenant, that everyone should let his male servant, and everyone his female servant go free, that no one should make bondservants of them any more. They obeyed and let them go;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: but afterwards they turned, and caused the servants and the handmaids, whom they had let go free, to return, and brought them into subjection for servants and for handmaids.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Therefore Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says: ‘I made a covenant with your fathers in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of bondage, saying,
verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text: At the end of seven years, every man of you shall release his brother who is a Hebrew, who has been sold to you, and has served you six years. You shall let him go free from you; but your fathers didn’t listen to me, and didn’t incline their ear.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: You had now turned, and had done that which is right in my eyes, in every man proclaiming liberty to his neighbor. You had made a covenant before me in the house which is called by my name;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: but you turned and profaned my name, and every man caused his servant, and every man his handmaid, whom you had let go free at their pleasure, to return. You brought them into subjection, to be to you for servants and for handmaids.’”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Therefore Yahweh says: “You have not listened to me, to proclaim liberty, every man to his brother, and every man to his neighbor. Behold, I proclaim to you a liberty,” says Yahweh, “to the sword, to the pestilence, and to the famine. I will make you be tossed back and forth among all the kingdoms of the earth.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: I will give the men who have transgressed my covenant, who have not performed the words of the covenant which they made before me, when they cut the calf in two and passed between its parts:
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: the princes of Judah, the princes of Jerusalem, the eunuchs, the priests, and all the people of the land, who passed between the parts of the calf;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: I will even give them into the hand of their enemies, and into the hand of those who seek their life. Their dead bodies will be food for the birds of the sky and for the animals of the earth.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “I will give Zedekiah king of Judah and his princes into the hands of their enemies, into the hands of those who seek their life and into the hands of the king of Babylon’s army, who has gone away from you.
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: Behold, I will command,” says Yahweh, “and cause them to return to this city. They will fight against it, take it, and burn it with fire. I will make the cities of Judah a desolation, without inhabitant.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

explainer.summary: The Rechabites are commended for their faithfulness to their ancestor's commands (no wine, no houses), contrasting sharply with Judah's disobedience to God's commands.
explainer.contextInBook: The Rechabites obey a human ancestor more faithfully than Judah obeys God -- a devastating comparison.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: The word which came to Jeremiah from Yahweh in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Go to the house of the Rechabites, and speak to them, and bring them into Yahweh’s house, into one of the rooms, and give them wine to drink.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then I took Jaazaniah the son of Jeremiah, the son of Habazziniah, with his brothers, all his sons, and the whole house of the Rechabites;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: and I brought them into Yahweh’s house, into the room of the sons of Hanan the son of Igdaliah, the man of God, which was by the room of the princes, which was above the room of Maaseiah the son of Shallum, the keeper of the threshold.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: I set before the sons of the house of the Rechabites bowls full of wine, and cups; and I said to them, “Drink wine!”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: But they said, “We will drink no wine; for Jonadab the son of Rechab, our father, commanded us, saying, ‘You shall drink no wine, neither you, nor your sons, forever.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: You shall not build a house, sow seed, plant a vineyard, or have any; but all your days you shall dwell in tents, that you may live many days in the land in which you live as nomads.’
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: We have obeyed the voice of Jonadab the son of Rechab, our father, in all that he commanded us, to drink no wine all our days, we, our wives, our sons, or our daughters;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: and not to build houses for ourselves to dwell in. We have no vineyard, field, or seed;
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: but we have lived in tents, and have obeyed, and done according to all that Jonadab our father commanded us.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: But when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up into the land, we said, ‘Come, and let us go to Jerusalem for fear of the army of the Chaldeans, and for fear of the army of the Syrians; so we will dwell at Jerusalem.’”
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: Then Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Go and tell the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, “Will you not receive instruction to listen to my words?” says Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “The words of Jonadab the son of Rechab that he commanded his sons, not to drink wine, are performed; and to this day they drink none, for they obey their father’s commandment; but I have spoken to you, rising up early and speaking, and you have not listened to me.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: I have sent also to you all my servants the prophets, rising up early and sending them, saying, ‘Every one of you must return now from his evil way, amend your doings, and don’t go after other gods to serve them, then you will dwell in the land which I have given to you and to your fathers:’ but you have not inclined your ear, nor listened to me.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: The sons of Jonadab the son of Rechab have performed the commandment of their father which he commanded them, but this people has not listened to me.”’
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Therefore Yahweh, the God of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Behold, I will bring on Judah and on all the inhabitants of Jerusalem all the evil that I have pronounced against them; because I have spoken to them, but they have not heard; and I have called to them, but they have not answered.’”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Jeremiah said to the house of the Rechabites, “Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Because you have obeyed the commandment of Jonadab your father, and kept all his precepts, and done according to all that he commanded you;’
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: therefore Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘Jonadab the son of Rechab will not lack a man to stand before me forever.’”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

explainer.summary: Jeremiah dictates his prophecies to Baruch, who reads them publicly. King Jehoiakim cuts the scroll apart and burns it in a fire. Jeremiah dictates a new scroll with even more words.
explainer.contextInBook: Jehoiakim's burning of the scroll symbolizes the nation's rejection of God's word -- but the word survives.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: In the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, this word came to Jeremiah from Yahweh, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Take a scroll of a book, and write in it all the words that I have spoken to you against Israel, and against Judah, and against all the nations, from the day I spoke to you, from the days of Josiah, even to this day.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the evil which I intend to do to them; that they may each return from his evil way; that I may forgive their iniquity and their sin.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Neriah; and Baruch wrote from the mouth of Jeremiah all Yahweh’s words, which he had spoken to him, on a scroll of a book.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Jeremiah commanded Baruch, saying, “I am restricted. I can’t go into Yahweh’s house.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Therefore you go, and read from the scroll which you have written from my mouth, Yahweh’s words, in the ears of the people in Yahweh’s house on the fast day. Also you shall read them in the ears of all Judah who come out of their cities.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: It may be they will present their supplication before Yahweh, and will each return from his evil way; for Yahweh has pronounced great anger and wrath against this people.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Baruch the son of Neriah did according to all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him, reading in the book Yahweh’s words in Yahweh’s house.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now in the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah, king of Judah, in the ninth month, all the people in Jerusalem, and all the people who came from the cities of Judah to Jerusalem, proclaimed a fast before Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Baruch read the words of Jeremiah from the book in Yahweh’s house, in the room of Gemariah the son of Shaphan, the scribe, in the upper court, at the entry of the new gate of Yahweh’s house, in the ears of all the people.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: When Micaiah the son of Gemariah, the son of Shaphan, had heard out of the book all Yahweh’s words,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: he went down into the king’s house, into the scribe’s room: and behold, all the princes were sitting there, Elishama the scribe, Delaiah the son of Shemaiah, Elnathan the son of Achbor, Gemariah the son of Shaphan, Zedekiah the son of Hananiah, and all the princes.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Micaiah declared to them all the words that he had heard, when Baruch read the book in the ears of the people.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
Therefore all the princes sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah, the son of Shelemiah, the son of Cushi, to Baruch, saying, “Take in your hand the scroll in which you have read in the ears of the people, and come.”
So Baruch the son of Neriah took the scroll in his hand, and came to them.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
They said to him, “Sit down now, and read it in our hearing.”
So Baruch read it in their hearing.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now when they had heard all the words, they turned in fear one toward another, and said to Baruch, “We will surely tell the king of all these words.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: They asked Baruch, saying, “Tell us now, how did you write all these words at his mouth?”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Baruch answered them, “He dictated all these words to me with his mouth, and I wrote them with ink in the book.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then the princes said to Baruch, “You and Jeremiah go hide. Don’t let anyone know where you are.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: They went in to the king into the court; but they had laid up the scroll in the room of Elishama the scribe. Then they told all the words in the hearing of the king.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: So the king sent Jehudi to get the scroll; and he took it out of the room of Elishama the scribe. Jehudi read it in the hearing of the king, and in the hearing of all the princes who stood beside the king.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now the king was sitting in the winter house in the ninth month; and there was a fire in the brazier burning before him.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: When Jehudi had read three or four leaves, the king cut it with the penknife, and cast it into the fire that was in the brazier, until all the scroll was consumed in the fire that was in the brazier.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: The king and his servants who heard all these words were not afraid, and didn’t tear their garments.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Moreover Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah had made intercession to the king that he would not burn the scroll; but he would not listen to them.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: The king commanded Jerahmeel the king’s son, and Seraiah the son of Azriel, and Shelemiah the son of Abdeel, to arrest Baruch the scribe and Jeremiah the prophet; but Yahweh hid them.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Yahweh’s word came to Jeremiah, after the king had burned the scroll, and the words which Baruch wrote at the mouth of Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Take again another scroll, and write in it all the former words that were in the first scroll, which Jehoiakim the king of Judah has burned.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah you shall say, ‘Yahweh says: “You have burned this scroll, saying, “Why have you written therein, saying, ‘The king of Babylon will certainly come and destroy this land, and will cause to cease from there man and animal?’”’
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: Therefore Yahweh says concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: “He will have no one to sit on David’s throne. His dead body will be cast out in the day to the heat, and in the night to the frost.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: I will punish him, his offspring, and his servants for their iniquity. I will bring on them, on the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and on the men of Judah, all the evil that I have pronounced against them, but they didn’t listen.”’”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then took Jeremiah another scroll, and gave it to Baruch the scribe, the son of Neriah, who wrote therein from the mouth of Jeremiah all the words of the book which Jehoiakim king of Judah had burned in the fire; and many similar words were added to them.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

explainer.summary: During a temporary lifting of the Babylonian siege, Jeremiah tries to leave Jerusalem and is arrested as a deserter. He is beaten and imprisoned in a dungeon.
explainer.contextInBook: Jeremiah's physical suffering intensifies as the political crisis deepens.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: Zedekiah the son of Josiah reigned as king, instead of Coniah the son of Jehoiakim, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon made king in the land of Judah.
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: But neither he, nor his servants, nor the people of the land, listened to Yahweh’s words, which he spoke by the prophet Jeremiah.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Zedekiah the king sent Jehucal the son of Shelemiah, and Zephaniah the son of Maaseiah, the priest, to the prophet Jeremiah, saying, “Pray now to Yahweh our God for us.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now Jeremiah came in and went out among the people; for they had not put him into prison.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Pharaoh’s army had come out of Egypt; and when the Chaldeans who were besieging Jerusalem heard news of them, they broke up from Jerusalem.
verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text: Then Yahweh’s word came to the prophet Jeremiah, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Yahweh, the God of Israel, says, ‘You shall tell the king of Judah, who sent you to me to inquire of me: “Behold, Pharaoh’s army, which has come out to help you, will return to Egypt into their own land.
verses.explainer: Egypt represents the temptation to flee God's discipline rather than endure it -- running from judgment rather than through it.

verses.text: The Chaldeans will come again, and fight against this city. They will take it and burn it with fire.”’
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Yahweh says, ‘Don’t deceive yourselves, saying, “The Chaldeans will surely depart from us;” for they will not depart.
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: For though you had struck the whole army of the Chaldeans who fight against you, and there remained but wounded men among them, yes they would each rise up in his tent and burn this city with fire.’”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: When the army of the Chaldeans had broken up from Jerusalem for fear of Pharaoh’s army,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: then Jeremiah went out of Jerusalem to go into the land of Benjamin, to receive his portion there, in the middle of the people.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: When he was in Benjamin’s gate, a captain of the guard was there, whose name was Irijah, the son of Shelemiah, the son of Hananiah; and he seized Jeremiah the prophet, saying, “You are defecting to the Chaldeans!”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
Then Jeremiah said, “That is false! I am not defecting to the Chaldeans.”
But he didn’t listen to him; so Irijah siezed Jeremiah, and brought him to the princes.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: The princes were angry with Jeremiah, and struck him, and put him in prison in the house of Jonathan the scribe; for they had made that the prison.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: When Jeremiah had come into the dungeon house, and into the cells, and Jeremiah had remained there many days,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
then Zedekiah the king sent, and had him brought out. The king asked him secretly in his house, “Is there any word from Yahweh?”
Jeremiah said, “There is.” He also said, “You will be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Moreover Jeremiah said to king Zedekiah, “How have I sinned against you, against your servants, or against this people, that you have put me in prison?
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now where are your prophets who prophesied to you, saying, ‘The king of Babylon will not come against you, nor against this land?’
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: Now please hear, my lord the king: please let my supplication be presented before you, that you not cause me to return to the house of Jonathan the scribe, lest I die there.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Zedekiah the king commanded, and they committed Jeremiah into the court of the guard. They gave him daily a loaf of bread out of the bakers’ street, until all the bread in the city was gone. Thus Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

explainer.summary: Officials throw Jeremiah into a muddy cistern to die. Ebed-Melech, an Ethiopian court official, rescues him. Jeremiah secretly tells Zedekiah to surrender, but the king is too afraid of his own officials to obey.
explainer.contextInBook: Ebed-Melech, a foreigner, shows more courage and compassion than the king of Judah.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed wi
wordStudies.meaning: A binding agreement between two parties, sealed with promises and obligations.
wordStudies.significance: Jeremiah's message is framed by covenant: Judah broke the old covenant, and God promises to establish a new one.

verses.text: Shephatiah the son of Mattan, and Gedaliah the son of Pashhur, and Jucal the son of Shelemiah, and Pashhur the son of Malchijah, heard the words that Jeremiah spoke to all the people, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “Yahweh says, ‘He who remains in this city will die by the sword, by the famine, and by the pestilence; but he who goes out to the Chaldeans will live, and he will escape with his life, and he will live.’
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: Yahweh says, ‘This city will surely be given into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon, and he will take it.’”
verses.explainer: The prophetic messenger formula introduces a direct word from God through His prophet Jeremiah.

verses.text: Then the princes said to the king, “Please let this man be put to death; because he weakens the hands of the men of war who remain in this city, and the hands of all the people, in speaking such words to them: for this man doesn’t seek the welfare of this people, but harm.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Zedekiah the king said, “Behold, he is in your hand; for the king can’t do anything to oppose you.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then took they Jeremiah and threw him into the dungeon of Malchijah the king’s son, that was in the court of the guard. They let down Jeremiah with cords. In the dungeon there was no water, but mire; and Jeremiah sank in the mire.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Now when Ebedmelech the Ethiopian, a eunuch, who was in the king’s house, heard that they had put Jeremiah in the dungeon (the king was then sitting in Benjamin’s gate),
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Ebedmelech went out of the king’s house, and spoke to the king, saying,
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: “My lord the king, these men have done evil in all that they have done to Jeremiah the prophet, whom they have cast into the dungeon. He is likely to die in the place where he is, because of the famine; for there is no more bread in the city.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then the king commanded Ebedmelech the Ethiopian, saying, “Take from here thirty men with you, and take up Jeremiah the prophet out of the dungeon, before he dies.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: So Ebedmelech took the men with him, and went into the house of the king under the treasury, and took from there rags and worn-out garments, and let them down by cords into the dungeon to Jeremiah.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
Ebedmelech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah, “Now put these rags and worn-out garments under your armpits under the cords.”
Jeremiah did so.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: So they drew up Jeremiah with the cords, and took him up out of the dungeon; and Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Zedekiah the king sent and took Jeremiah the prophet to himself into the third entry that is in Yahweh’s house. Then the king said to Jeremiah, “I will ask you something. Hide nothing from me.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah, “If I declare it to you, will you not surely put me to death? If I give you counsel, you will not listen to me.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: So Zedekiah the king swore secretly to Jeremiah, saying, “As Yahweh lives, who made us this soul, I will not put you to death, neither will I give you into the hand of these men who seek your life.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah, “Yahweh, the God of Armies, the God of Israel, says: ‘If you will go out to the king of Babylon’s princes, then your soul will live, and this city will not be burned with fire. You will live, along with your house.
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: But if you will not go out to the king of Babylon’s princes, then this city will be given into the hand of the Chaldeans, and they will burn it with fire, and you won’t escape out of their hand.’”
verses.explainer: Babylon is God's chosen instrument of judgment on Judah, and Jeremiah's unpopular message was to submit to Babylonian authority rather than resist.

verses.text: Zedekiah the king said to Jeremiah, “I am afraid of the Jews who have defected to the Chaldeans, lest they deliver me into their hand, and they mock me.”
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: But Jeremiah said, “They won’t deliver you. Obey, I beg you, Yahweh’s voice, in that which I speak to you; so it will be well with you, and your soul will live.
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text: But if you refuse to go out, this is the word that Yahweh has shown me:
verses.explainer: The fall of Jerusalem unfolds as Jeremiah prophesied, and the surviving remnant faces choices that will determine their future.

verses.text:
